title: KBUtilLib → BERIL augmentation: deployer + /kbu primer + kbu-notebook + kbu-fba

modules.name: skill bundle (/kbu, kbu-notebook, kbu-fba) + preferences.md + util.py templates
jupyter-dev supersede (home-repo source)
modules.path: (home_repo source — look up in skill registry)

key_decisions: Track upstream BERIL; never fork. Deploy untracked skills + gitignored config; survives beril start release re-checkout (verified).
KBUtilLib owns a CRAFT-style deployer; does not join CRAFT. Separate distribution channel from claude-skills sync.
kbu beril install pip-installs kbutillib into the BERIL env (idempotent, PEP-668 fallback, env-detect); doctor verifies import + version.
3 deployed units: /kbu primer (manual), kbu-notebook, kbu-fba (auto-discoverable). Preferences in .claude/kbu/preferences.md.
kbu-fba covers full arc reconstruct->gapfill->analyze; run_fva not cobra FVA.

kbu-notebook = %run util.py + NotebookSession.for_notebook + cell independence + cache-as-you-go + portability; supersedes jupyter-dev; maps onto BERIL projects/{id}/ dirs; scoped to local COBRA/MSModelUtil modeling notebooks.

Graduated execution policy: green run-free / yellow sample-then-consult on ANY of {runtime>threshold, self-flagged uncertainty, large fan-out, compute-intensive} / red full-run on sign-off, user picks where. Thresholds in preferences.md.
Manifest SPLIT: keep provenance-DAG reads; drop org/run-state (Manifest.render) role.
tests: Module 1 deployer: temp fake BERIL root — untracked deploy, preferences render-if-absent + never-clobber, idempotent re-install, version-match pip skip, doctor green/red, survive simulated git checkout.
Module 3 NotebookSession: for_notebook resolves storage root + .kbcache onto BERIL projects/{id}/ shape; provenance reads without kbu-project.toml.
Module 2 skill smoke: each SKILL.md parses, util.py template imports, preferences.md has threshold keys.
out_of_scope: PRD B kbu-harness (container repo, rsync, design-deploy skill, in-harness execution, MD dev-log).
Upstream contribution into KBase BERIL; joining CRAFT; changing BERIL tracked files / berdl_start onboarding; BERIL Spark/query notebook conventions.
